AgentKey is a plugin that connects your agent to live external data in one command. Install it into Claude Code, Codex, OpenClaw, or any MCP-based agent and instantly unlock access to search, web pages, social platforms, finance, e-commerce, business and crypto data. No integrations. No setup. Auto failover keeps workflows running.

Blocks.ai

Blocks.aiThe Control Plane & Network Layer For AI Agents [...] You wire up one API for search, another for scraping, another for social, another for market data. You babysit a pile of keys and bills, and you basically have to understand the plumbing before you can even start. Most people who'd benefit from this never get past that part. So we built AgentKey. You install it like a plugin, and your agent gets access to a whole marketplace of capabilities behind a single account. No juggling APIs, no keys to manage, no need to be technical. One account, one bill, and your agent suddenly gets a lot more powerful. Right now the marketplace is organized into a few tiers, and it keeps growing: - Everyday tools: search, web scraping, social media - Professional data: finance, crypto, business data - Life and travel: weather, maps, trip planning, and more It [...] Hi Product Hunt, I'm Mogu, founder of Chainbase. Today I'm really happy to share the newest project out of our lab: AgentKey. It's been in private beta for a while, and a few thousand people have been using it and helping us shape it along the way, so it finally feels ready to put in front of you here. Here's the thing we kept coming back to. Agents have gotten genuinely good at doing tasks. What they don't have is eyes. They can reason and act, but they can't actually see the live internet, which is where almost all the useful tools and real-time data actually live. So you end up with a strange gap: an agent smart enough to do the work, sitting behind a window it can't look through. And today, giving an agent those eyes is a chore. You wire up one API for search, another for scraping,

## Latest commit

AgentKey

AgentKey

One command. Full internet access for your AI agent.
  
Browse Twitter, search LinkedIn, scrape social media, read any webpage. Zero config. Just install and go.

Install ·
Platforms ·
FAQ ·
中文

Website
Console

Website
Console

Install AgentKey. Give your AI superpowers.

AgentKey is the master key for the agent ecosystem. When using Claude, Manus, or other agents, you often need external data: social media, e-commerce, on-chain data, various APIs. That means hunting down API keys, managing subscriptions, or hitting dead ends.

With AgentKey installed, your agent gains all these data capabilities automatically. No subscriptions, no extra registrations. Top up and go. [...] Before AgentKey: 10 tasks → 10 API keys → 10 separate bills.

Your agent is half-capable at best, constantly needing human help to find data, juggling credentials, drowning in complexity.

Now: one AgentKey handles everything. AgentKey unifies all the external access your AI needs to do real work.

## New here? Start on the web

Before touching the terminal, you can get a feel for AgentKey directly in your browser — the website and console explain things more visually than this README can.

The one-line install below is what plugs AgentKey into your AI agent. If you only want to look around first, the two links above are the friendlier starting point.

## Install [...] Underlying providers are routed automatically and grow over time — your agent can call `find_tools` to see what's currently available.

`find_tools`

Planned: Maps & Weather

## FAQ

Yes. AgentKey is a master key — one platform that unlocks external capabilities for your agent. By design, we have no access to your local files, your credentials, or your agent's conversations. The only data AgentKey collects is anonymous usage telemetry — which agent you installed into, your skill version, and upgrade outcomes — never your queries or responses. See "How do I opt out of telemetry?" below.

Native web access in Claude and ChatGPT has limited platform coverage. It often can't reach Twitter, on-chain data, etc. AgentKey fills those gaps.

Just top up. No auto-renewal, no hidden charges.

results.title: Introducing AgentKey by Chainbase Labs
results.content:
Cover photo

# Introducing AgentKey by Chainbase Labs

Today we're launching AgentKey. Our mission is simple: connect agents to the world.

AgentKey is a new product from Chainbase Labs. One install gives any AI agent access to the full internet. It works with Claude Code, OpenClaw, Hermes, Cursor CLI, and 10+ other agent runtimes. Available to everyone starting today.

### The Handless Agent Era

Models keep getting smarter, and agents can now complete more tasks end to end. But a smart model doesn't automatically make a capable agent.

The messy workaround has looked the same in every team we've talked to.

You want your agent to check trends on TikTok? Sign up for a scraper. Top up the balance. Generate an API key. Paste it into a config file. Pray it still works in a week. [...] No provider accounts to register, no subscriptions, no keys to manage. One balance covers every call. You pay per use, and most requests cost a fraction of a cent. New platforms get added continuously, and every connected agent gets them the moment they ship.

### How we built it

AI agents are the new user. Every design decision in AgentKey starts there.

Usage-based pricing, no subscriptions. Your agent uses what it uses. You pay for that. [...] The hardest part isn't any one source. It's all of them. Anyone can wire up a single API. But an agent doesn't know in advance whether it'll need TikTok, a Solana wallet, a Korean news site, or a Reddit thread from 2019. AgentKey covers the full surface, social platforms, on-chain activity, regional sources outside the English-speaking web, behind one interface.

The agent shouldn't have to choose. Which provider to use, when to switch, what to do when something fails: those are decisions we handle. Every category (search, scraping, social, crypto) sits behind multiple providers with the same interface. If one has a bad hour, AgentKey quietly switches behind the scenes. Your agent doesn't stall, doesn't make up the answer. It just keeps going.

### What's coming next

No monthly platform fee. Top up once, spend down as your agent searches, reads pages, or pulls social. Typical lookups cost cents — see FAQ for ballpark numbers.

Credits Cost $3.55

web.search × 120 $1.40

twitter.data × 450 $1.35

chainbase.rpc × 658 $0.80

One wallet for every kind of lookup [...] Which platforms does it work with? +

Claude Code is ready today; more assistants are rolling out. If your tool runs plugins or skills and can follow install instructions, it can use AgentKey.

Why not just sign up for each API myself? +

You could — that means separate bills, OAuth screens, and downtime per vendor. AgentKey is the “one stop” path: one balance, one setup story, and we reroute when a source is flaky so your session is not derailed.

## AgentKey is live. Plug your agent in.

One key for search, scrape, social and crypto APIs. Pay as you go — no subscription, no setup.

Try it for free [...] What can my agent do with AgentKey? −

Everyday research: what people say on X or Reddit, how a product is reviewed, what a long article says, token prices, GitHub readme summaries — the gaps native “browse the web” often misses. One AgentKey covers those fetches so you are not opening ten vendor sites yourself.

Do I need to write any code? +

No. Create an account, paste the install command from our Quick Install, and let the agent walk through the prompts. If you can copy and paste, you are qualified.

How much does it cost? +

Pay-as-you-go: no monthly platform fee. Ballpark from live usage — web search ~$0.001, token lookup ~$0.003, a social read ~$0.006. Heavy daily automation can land around a few dollars to ten per month. Top up any time; no hidden auto-renew.
